title: Adding with arrays
description: Practice solving equations by adding the same number many times.
topic_path: Topic A, C, D, & E: Foundations

hints:
There are $\blueE4\purpleD{\text{ twos}}$.

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{2}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{2}} + \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{2}}+ \overset{\blueE4}{\purpleD{2}}$
This image shows $\blueE4$ rows with $\purpleD{2}$ in each row.

![4 rows of squares. Each row has 2 squares. The rows are labeled 1 through 4 at the left and the columns are labeled 1 and 2 at the top.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ba256e6006c0e2d25db1dc0ea6662f3396a1af8)
This image represents $2 + 2 + 2 + 2$:

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choices describe the number of penguins?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9f5ec44c30d2b3bb62ba452d31e71bab88ac55ad)

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueE4$ rows with $\purpleD3$ penguins in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/34be25095efa0b9bd1c09e890f7bbddeb2ab9c9b)
$\blueE4$ rows of $\purpleD3$ is equal to $\overset{\blueE1}\purpleD3+\overset{\blueE2}\purpleD3+\overset{\blueE3}\purpleD3+\overset{\blueE4}\purpleD3$.

We can also think of the penguins in $\purpleD3$ columns with $\blueE4$ penguins in each column.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29deea13a095bf412858c09d6a18228ec500b148)
$\purpleD3$ columns of $\blueE4$ is equal to $\overset{\purpleD1}\blueE4 + \overset{\purpleD2}\blueE4 + \overset{\purpleD3}\blueE4$.

The number of penguins is equal to:  

* $3+3+3+3$
* $4+4+4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choices describe the number of flowers?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d248f2a9f4668439fea22d8e573772d40af6cff4) 

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD3$ flowers in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d3c6ee0fc55474330ba002b3b24001cc1cd1219)
$\blueE2$ rows of $\purpleD3$ is equal to $\overset{\blueE1}\purpleD3+\overset{\blueE2}\purpleD3$.

We can also think of the flowers in $\purpleD3$ columns with $\blueE2$ flowers in each column.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90060f7c8468d5f12c5d2fc8d840383c10baea08)
$\purpleD3$ columns of $\blueE2$ is equal to $\overset{\purpleD1}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD2}\blueE2+ \overset{\purpleD3}\blueE2$.

The number of flowers is equal to:  

* $3+3$
* $2+2+2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choices describe the number of acorns?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8df3ac756c8405b561bff77d2606faca43c6aecc)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE4$ rows with $\purpleD5$ acorns in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/96781fa7b221f19d452a49b18c3c46a96d56718b)
$\blueE4$ rows of $\purpleD5$ is equal to $\overset{\blueE1}\purpleD5+\overset{\blueE2}\purpleD5 +\overset{\blueE3}\purpleD5+\overset{\blueE4}\purpleD5$.

We can also think of the acorns in $\purpleD5$ columns with $\blueE4$ acorns in each column.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5bb47106a2563d9be65dd308b148eb9a5f361629)
$\purpleD5$ columns of $\blueE4$ is equal to $\overset{\purpleD1}\blueE4 + \overset{\purpleD2}\blueE4+ \overset{\purpleD3}\blueE4 + \overset{\purpleD4}\blueE4 + \overset{\purpleD5}\blueE4$.

The number of acorns is equal to:  

* $5 + 5 + 5 + 5$
* $4 + 4 + 4 + 4 +4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choices describe the number of cheese wedges?**


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a9c2e9667316864fb5d3dfdf389dadd921bca053)

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD5$ cheese wedges in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ba7f193e26e763392f487256da49c1df26ec60bc)
$\blueE2$ rows of $\purpleD5$ is equal to $\overset{\blueE1}\purpleD5+\overset{\blueE2}\purpleD5$.

We can also think of the cheese wedges in $\purpleD5$ columns with $\blueE2$ cheese wedges in each column.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4fd07af2151219b27a24a90b912298317ab258be)
$\purpleD5$ columns of $\blueE2$ is equal to $\overset{\purpleD1}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD2}\blueE2+ \overset{\purpleD3}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD4}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD5}\blueE2$.

The number of cheese wedges is equal to:  

* $5+5$ 
* $2+2+2+2+2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choices describe the number of squares?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/886f75282588f813a517774d3f58e60ebe5cb711)

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD4$ squares in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d3e56bb8caddfed34574d886c3cb50c03417d64d)
$\blueE2$ rows of $\purpleD4$ is equal to $\overset{\blueE1}\purpleD4+\overset{\blueE2}\purpleD4$.

We can also think of the squares in $\purpleD4$ columns with $\blueE2$ squares in each column.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8191d6468c8ea9282b5d1a51f06b4814375d2d4e)
$\purpleD4$ columns of $\blueE2$ is equal to $\overset{\purpleD1}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD2}\blueE2+ \overset{\purpleD3}\blueE2 + \overset{\purpleD4}\blueE2$.

The number of squares is equal to:  

* $2+2+2+2$
* $4+4$
